---
title: "Invited Speakers"
page-layout: article
toc: false
---

The **First National Seminar on Bioactive Molecules** will feature distinguished researchers and experts from academia, research institutions, and industry.

The list of invited speakers will be updated as confirmations are received.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Keynote Speakers

::: callout-note
### Coming Soon

The keynote speakers will be announced shortly.

Stay tuned for updates!
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Invited Speakers

::::::: grid
::: g-col-md-6
### 👤 Speaker 1

**Name:** To be announced

**Affiliation:** —

**Country:** —

**Topic:** —
:::

::: g-col-md-6
### 👤 Speaker 2

**Name:** To be announced

**Affiliation:** —

**Country:** —

**Topic:** —
:::

::: g-col-md-6
### 👤 Speaker 3

**Name:** To be announced

**Affiliation:** —

**Country:** —

**Topic:** —
:::

::: g-col-md-6
### 👤 Speaker 4

**Name:** To be announced

**Affiliation:** —

**Country:** —

**Topic:** —
:::
:::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Scientific Themes

The invited talks will cover the seminar's five thematic areas:

-   🩺 Pharmacology of Bioactive Molecules and Citizen Health
-   🌱 Bioactive Molecules for Food Security and Agro-industry
-   🔬 Microbial Biotechnologies and Energy Security
-   🌍 Environmental Valorization and Preservation
-   🤖 Digital Innovation and Artificial Intelligence in Biotechnology

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Call for Speakers

Researchers interested in delivering an invited lecture may contact the organizing committee.

**Email:**\
**nsbm\@ensbiotech.edu.dz**

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: callout-important
## Speaker Information

Each invited speaker will have a dedicated profile including:

-   Photograph
-   Short biography
-   Affiliation
-   Research interests
-   Presentation title
-   Abstract
:::
